title: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene
display_title: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene
wikitext:
[]: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by IntrovertedAnon » Sun Nov 12, 2023 2:20 pm I got this from a friend and they showed me this screenshot of the script during the train station scene on page 379. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sun Nov 12, 2023 7:56 pm Yeah I asked a translator and it just says "Rei and Kaworu at the station". I guess "couple" is just a machine translation artefact.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 13, 2023 9:09 am I love Shin Eva’s energy. Mari X Asuka = RED HERRING. Shinji X Asuka = FAILED. Shinji X Rei = ABSOLUTELY NOT. Rei X Kaworu = NOT CANON. Shinji X Mari = TEASED.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by AlphaZero » Mon Nov 13, 2023 10:51 am After tinkering with the text in both Google and Deepl, looking up every word and interpreting it. This is a translation I came up with, it can be wrong though: On Platform 1 across Shinji there's a male-female couple. The close by couple, Rei and Kaworu, is obstructed by an incoming train. Was struggling with "ごし" but it seems to be "越し". The word "シャッター" can also be translated as camera shutter but it makes no sense here instead it means shutter like window shutter. The word for couple "カップル" does mean romantic couple instead of just two individuals.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Mon Nov 13, 2023 11:46 am FelipeFritschF wrote: Yeah I asked a translator and it just says "Rei and Kaworu at the station". I guess "couple" is just a machine translation artefact.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Nov 13, 2023 1:41 pm I've thrown the thread Nuke's way, so we can get an actual, authoritative answer on this instead of secondhand accounts and trying to divine meaning from machine translations

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Nov 13, 2023 4:07 pm The script addressing Kaworu and Rei as 男女 would be very much on point, if the specific line is referring to that cut of them on the other side of the station. At that point of the story they matter so little to Shinji they are just "a man and a woman".

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Nov 13, 2023 5:43 pm Joseki wrote: The script addressing Kaworu and Rei as 男女 would be very much on point, if the specific line is referring to that cut of them on the other side of the station. At that point of the story they matter so little to Shinji they are just "a man and a woman".

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Nov 13, 2023 8:58 pm Blockio wrote: I've thrown the thread Nuke's way, so we can get an actual, authoritative answer on this instead of secondhand accounts and trying to divine meaning from machine translations

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Konja7 » Tue Nov 14, 2023 12:07 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: "Track 1 platform across from Shinji." Self-explanatory. The track 1 platform across from Shinji. "Multiple M/F couples." 男女 is a catch-all for both sexes, カップル is the English word for romantic couples on loan to Japanese for the day, and ら is a pluralizer that gives us couples. This is a strange note because in the actual scene, there are two girls standing on their own, one girl who appears to be Asuka, one gentleman with male pattern baldness, and then Rei and Kaworu. Maybe this direction was changed at some point during production, but that's what the Japanese says: multiple couples of men and women.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 14, 2023 12:22 am Yes, Asuka doesn't appear in the screenshot of the script at all. Interesting that Kaworu and Rei are highlighted, but not everyone's favorite Evangelion redhead. The whole reason I went out of my way to say that I didn't want to translate 寄り as "among them" is because it has the meaning of "like" or "similar to" in English. If you count yourself among the members of a group, you're saying you're similar to them. But in Japanese, it measures proximity. While you can convey this with "among them", it opens up the translation for questions like, "Does it mean they're close to the couples, or that they are a couple themselves like the others?" That is precisely the reason whyI didn't use that word in my translation and used "near them" which leaves fewer opportunities to interpret the English in ways the Japanese doesn't afford.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Nov 14, 2023 12:48 am Thank you, Nuke, that helps. Of course, while I know translators myself they don't have forum accounts nor are they interested in going too deep into this so I just quickly asked them what they thought and they didn't want to say much.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by nerv bae » Tue Nov 14, 2023 8:08 am Konja7 wrote: It's interesting that Asuka doesn't seem to be part of the script. If the character is Asuka, her appearance in the train station is really weird. Unlike Rei and Kaworu, we don't have a close-up to the character. Not to mention that her appearance in the train station don't fit her ending in the Instrumentality (where she returns to Earth).

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Nov 14, 2023 11:22 am nerv bae wrote: Must be Original Asuka at the train station, then. After all, the film has two Asukas to dispose of!

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Nov 14, 2023 11:56 am It rather neatly gives a reason for why she doesn't get much focus at all when she logically should if she's Asuka but not our (or Shinji's) Asuka.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Nov 14, 2023 2:14 pm Konja7 wrote: If the character is Asuka, her appearance in the train station is really weird. Unlike Rei and Kaworu, we don't have a close-up to the character. Not to mention that her appearance in the train station don't fit her ending in the Instrumentality (where she returns to Earth).

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Konja7 » Tue Nov 14, 2023 3:24 pm kuribo-04 wrote: I think that's easily explained by the ending scene being largely symbolic. Mari was gonna get Shinji out of the Anti-Universe too.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Nov 14, 2023 4:36 pm Sort of disappointed at no Asuka mention in the script because I've long wondered if she's holding either a cellphone or handheld game, and across the net it seems there's been no consensus. It's interesting, given some discourse here about the heteronormativity (or otherwise...) of NTE, that the script was set to populate the scene with male/female couples but a deliberate choice was made to not do that ... perhaps in conscious effort to avoid messaging as such. Funny that I already glommed onto that suggestion regardless, though.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by IntrovertedAnon » Mon Nov 27, 2023 6:35 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Yes, Asuka doesn't appear in the screenshot of the script at all. Interesting that Kaworu and Rei are highlighted, but not everyone's favorite Evangelion redhead. The whole reason I went out of my way to say that I didn't want to translate 寄り as "among them" is because it has the meaning of "like" or "similar to" in English. If you count yourself among the members of a group, you're saying you're similar to them. But in Japanese, it measures proximity. While you can convey this with "among them", it opens up the translation for questions like, "Does it mean they're close to the couples, or that they are a couple themselves like the others?" That is precisely the reason whyI didn't use that word in my translation and used "near them" which leaves fewer opportunities to interpret the English in ways the Japanese doesn't afford.

[]: Re: The Script for Rei and Kaworu at the Train Scene             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Nov 27, 2023 8:38 pm IntrovertedAnon wrote: I have talked with a few Japanese people and they said that Kaworu and Rei are a couple in the script, which confuses me because you said that it’s pretty much ambiguous on if they are or not and the Japanese fans say that they are.
